entries.title: 15-year-old girl killed by hit-and-run driver while riding e-scooter in South Shore: police
entries.summary: <p>A 15-year-old girl was killed and a 13-year-old girl was injured after a driver slammed into the electric scooter they were riding in the South Shore neighborhood Saturday night, Chicago police said. </p><p>A witness to the crash flagged down police and told them a car hit the right side of the e-scooter carrying Violet Harris and the younger girl about 8:30 p.m. in the 2800 block of East 77th Street, according to the Cook County Medical Examiner's Office and a police report obtained by the Sun-Times.</p><p>Violet, unresponsive in the street, was taken by ambulance to the University of Chicago Medical Center with severe head and chest injuries. She was pronounced dead within the hour, authorities said. </p><p>The girl driving the scooter got up after the crash and walked west on 77th Street to Jackson Park Hospital. Suffering from lacerations, she was transferred to the University of Chicago.</p><p>Surveillance video captured the crash, showing the car hitting them and continuing south along South Exchange Avenue, the report said.</p><p>An autopsy Sunday didn't rule on the cause or manner of Violet's death, with final rulings pending further investigation.</p><p><a class="Link" href="https://www.gofundme.com/f/a-loving-farewell-for-violet-harris?cdn-cache=0" target="_blank" >A GoFundMe set up by Violet’s mother</a> said she was an honors student at Hyde Park Academy who "filled the room with smiles when she walked in and brought so much joy to everyone she came in contact with." </p><p>No one was in custody late Sunday as detectives investigated.</p>
entries.author: Sun-Times Wire

entries.title: Venezuelan migrant charged with murder of Loyola student Sheridan Gorman
entries.summary: <p>A Venezuelan migrant has been charged with the murder of Loyola University Chicago student Sheridan Gorman, police announced Sunday, prompting outrage from Trump administration officials who branded her killing as the result of sanctuary policies in Illinois. </p><p>Jose Medina, 25, faces charges of first-degree murder, attempted murder and aggravated discharge of a firearm for the March 19 attack near a pier at Tobey Prinz Beach, according to Chicago police. </p><p>Federal immigration authorities said U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement issued an arrest detainer naming Medina hours before police announced the charges. </p><p>The Department of Homeland Security said Medina was taken into custody by the U.S. Border Patrol in May 2023, released and then taken into custody again two months later for shoplifting. He was charged with misdemeanor retail theft in that case, accused of stealing $132.50 of merchandise from the State Street Macy's, county court records show. An outstanding warrant from that case is active.</p><p>Records show Medina was born in Venezuela and at one point lived in a temporary migrant shelter at the Leone Beach Park Field House, which closed in 2024. He was among thousands of migrants who arrived in Chicago, <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/immigration/2024/02/15/busing-migrants-chicago-texas-origins-start-donald-trump-tucker-carlson-ted-cruz" >many of them bused to the city by GOP Texas Gov. Greg Abbott</a> in protest of former President Joe Biden's border policies. </p><p>Acting Assistant DHS Secretary Lauren Bis claimed in a statement that Gorman was "failed by open border policies and sanctuary politicians" and urged Gov. JB Pritzker to not release Medina, who was scheduled to make his first court appearance Monday. </p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-floatRight> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Sheridan Gorman</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Provided</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>Pritzker's office did not have any immediate comment. </p><p>Legislation signed by Republican former Illinois Gov. Bruce Rauner limits cooperation between local police and federal immigration authorities. The Illinois TRUST Act generally bars police from sharing information with immigration agents or handing over people in custody unless a federal criminal warrant is issued, a policy long denigrated by President Donald Trump and other conservative critics.</p><p>Gorman, 18, and her friends took an early morning stroll Thursday just north of the Rogers Park campus, hoping to capture some photos with a view of the skyline and potentially the Northern Lights, her family said in a statement.</p><p>When they arrived, a masked gunman “emerged from the shadows” and fired a single shot as they fled, according to a Chicago police report. One of Gorman’s friends <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/crime/2026/03/20/chicago-loyola-sheridan-gorman-rogers-park-crime-shooting-murder" target="_blank" ><u>who witnessed the shooting</u></a> told the Chicago Sun-Times that “it didn’t seem like it was intentionally targeted toward any of us.”</p><p>The friends ran in different directions when the shot was fired, but returned when they saw Gorman on the ground. She had been shot in her torso and was <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/crime/2026/03/19/woman-18-shot-to-death-in-predawn-rogers-park-attack" target="_blank" ><u>pronounced dead at the scene</u></a>, officials said.</p><p>In a statement, Gorman's family said they were "gravely disappointed by the policies and failures that allowed this individual to remain in a position to commit this crime. </p><p>"When systems fail — whether through release decisions, lack of coordination, or unwillingness to act — the consequences are not abstract. They are real. And in our case, they are permanent," Gorman's family said. “This case must be prosecuted to the fullest extent of both state and federal law. There can be no gaps, no shortcuts, and no second chances that put others at risk. Accountability must be complete.”</p><p>The family insisted "we will not allow this to be dismissed as ‘wrong place, wrong time.’ This was not random misfortune. This was a violent and preventable act.”</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/crime/2026/03/20/chicago-loyola-sheridan-gorman-rogers-park-crime-shooting-murder" >Friend of slain Loyola student Sheridan Gorman says Rogers Park shooting didn’t feel ‘targeted’</a> </li> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/crime/2026/03/21/police-questioning-person-of-interest-in-fatal-shooting-of-18-year-old-loyola-student" >Police questioning person of interest in fatal shooting of 18-year-old Loyola student</a> </li> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/crime/2026/03/19/woman-18-shot-to-death-in-predawn-rogers-park-attack" >Loyola University student, 18, shot to death in predawn attack at Rogers Park beach</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p></p>

entries.title: 33 people injured when Chicago tour bus crashes in Green Bay
entries.summary: <p>Thirty-three passengers on a tour bus from Chicago were injured Sunday afternoon when it crashed in Green Bay, Wisconsin.</p><p>The bus, headed to a casino in Michigan, was carrying 54 people when it exited northbound Interstate 43 about 2:45 p.m., crossed the roadway and went over an embankment, according to Green Bay authorities. At least two people were critically injured.</p><p>Injured passengers were taken to one of four local hospitals. Others were taken to a Salvation Army community center.</p><p>The cause of the crash was under investigation late Sunday. The Wisconsin State Patrol was probing whether the bus experienced mechanical issues, and Green Bay police were determining whether the bus driver would face potential citations or charges.<br></p>
entries.author: Sun-Times Wire

entries.title: 3 people shot in South Shore
entries.summary: <p>Three people were shot in the South Shore neighborhood Sunday evening, leaving two in critical condition, according to Chicago police.</p><p>They were in the driveway of an apartment complex in the 6900 block of South South Shore Drive about 5:20 p.m. when an unknown person shot them, police said.</p><p>They were taken to the University of Chicago Medical Center. One, a 26-year-old man, was shot in the chest and listed in critical condition. A second male victim, whose age wasn’t reported, was also in critical condition with a gunshot wound to the neck. The third victim was in fair condition with an arm wound, police said.</p><p>No arrests were made, but a rifle and two handguns were recovered, police said. </p>
entries.author: Sun-Times Wire

entries.title: Judge rules Broadview protest curfew violates First Amendment
entries.summary: <p>A federal Judge on Sunday struck down the village of Broadview’s curfew on protests, ruling that restrictions set by suburban leaders have violated demonstrators' First Amendments rights. </p><p>Chicago attorney Robert Held filed for a preliminary injunction <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/news/2025/11/13/lawyer-seeking-preliminary-injunction-in-lawsuit-against-broadview-over-protest-hours-appears-outside-ice-facility" >last fall</a> as part of a federal lawsuit against Broadview to overturn Mayor Katrina Thompson’s executive order establishing time limits and designated areas for protests outside the Immigration and Customs Enforcement facility in Broadview.</p><p>U.S. District Judge Edmond Chang granted the preliminary injunction, writing in his decision that Broadview’s “current restrictions cannot stand under the First Amendment.”</p><p>Held called the ruling a “heartwarming victory.”</p><p>“The judge looked at the facts and the law and slapped down mayor,” Held told the Sun-Times. “This is a victory not just for the protesters, but anyone in this country who cares about the First Amendment and Constitution.”</p><p>The ICE property became the site <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/2025/11/14/scuffles-erupt-during-broadview-protest-several-arrested" >for major protests</a> against “Operation Midway Blitz,” the Trump administration’s aggressive deportation campaign in Chicago and its suburbs last year.</p><p>In response, Thompson issued several executive orders restricting where and when people can protest at the facility, which included establishing “free speech zones” and a daily curfew for demonstrations from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m.</p><p>Held filed the federal complaint in October against Thompson and Broadview Chief of Police Thomas Mills in an effort to overturn the village’s protest restrictions.</p><p>In granting the preliminary injunction, Chang said the ongoing daily limits on protests “fail to survive First Amendment scrutiny.” But the village is allowed to keep the protest zones and enact “more specific limits for specific protests on specific days.”</p><p>A Broadview spokesperson did not immediately respond a request for comment.</p><p><i>Contributing: Violet Miller</i> </p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" data-embed> <iframe src="https://docs.google.com/viewer?embedded=true&url=https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/6d/90/bf9520534073a8b9fdcd01ee8bdc/broadviewpiopinion.pdf"></iframe> </div> </div></div><p></p>
entries.author: Kaitlin Washburn

entries.title: White Sox sign catcher Reese McGuire, leaving Korey Lee as odd man out
entries.summary: <p>GLENDALE, Ariz. — The White Sox’ decision to sign catcher <b>Reese McGuire </b>to a one-year, $1.2 million contract was curious.</p><p><b>Kyle Teel</b> is rehabbing from a Grade 2 hamstring strain but is expected back early in the season, and<b> Korey Lee</b> and <b>Edgar Quero</b> are more than adequate.</p><p>But manager <b>Will Venable </b>said Lee wouldn’t be on the major-league roster.</p><p>‘‘You look at Quero moving into that top spot and how to best complement [McGuire], with Quero really doing most of his damage from the right side,’’ Venable said. ‘‘To be able to get a left-handed-hitting catcher to support [Quero] we thought was really important for us to get off to a good start.’’</p><p>Lee, whom the Sox acquired from the Astros at the 2023 trade deadline for reliever <b>Kendall Graveman</b>, was scheduled to catch Sunday against the Mariners. Lee was the Sox’ primary catcher in 2024, batting .210/.244/.347. He appeared in only 25 games last season after the Sox acquired Teel from the Red Sox and Quero established himself as a major-leaguer.</p><p>Lee’s work behind the plate was never as advanced as his hitting. He ranked near the bottom in most advanced fielding metrics.</p><p>Venable overlapped with McGuire in Boston in 2022, when Venable was the Red Sox’ bench coach.</p><p>‘‘He knows how to handle a pitching staff,’’ Venable said. ‘‘He knows how to navigate opposing hitters. Does a nice job at the plate. Has unlocked some power there recently.’’</p><p>McGuire returns for his second stint on the South Side, where he appeared in 53 games in 2022. McGuire, who was released by the Brewers on Sunday, played for the Cubs last season, hitting nine home runs in 133 at-bats.</p><p>The Sox are big on framing, and McGuire fits the bill after finishing in the 80th percentile in the metric last season, according to Baseball Savant.</p><p>Walking into the Sox’ facility, McGuire ran into familiar faces such as second baseman <b>Chase Meidroth</b> — McGuire’s brother <b>Shane </b>played with Meidroth in college — and former manager-turned-adviser <b>Tony La Russa</b>. He said wasn’t particularly choosy about which team to join.</p><p>‘‘Really any team is a good fit in my mind, right?’’ McGuire said.</p><p>It’ll be interesting to see what the Sox do once Teel returns. General manager <b>Chris Getz</b> said earlier in spring training that he didn’t want to carry three catchers, but the organization hasn’t thought that far ahead.</p><p>‘‘We’ll cross that road when we get there,’’ Venable said. ‘‘Right now, Kyle is not on the active roster, so we need to do everything we can to support this roster the best way.’’</p><h3>Roster moves</h3><p>The Sox optioned left-handed reliever <b>Brandon Eisert</b> to Triple-A Charlotte. He had a 4.39 ERA in 72 games with the Sox last season. The team likely will carry three lefty relievers in <b>Tyler Gilbert</b>, <b>Sean Newcomb</b> and <b>Chris Murphy</b>.</p><p>The Sox re-signed right-hander <b>Lucas Sims</b> to a minor-league contract. He pitched five scoreless innings this spring. The team has 36 players left in camp.</p>
entries.author: Kyle Williams

It's a good day to expand your horizons. Be patient during intense exchanges with partners and friends.</p><h2>Virgo (Aug. 23-Sept. 22)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> People notice you today. In fact, some will know personal details about your private life. (Be aware of this in case you have to do some damage control.) Meanwhile, this is an excellent day to settle disputes about shared property. Job-related issues or something to do with your health might be a concern.</p><h2>Libra (Sept. 23-Oct. 22)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Act on your urge to do something different so you can enjoy a little adventure. Go somewhere you've never been before. Meet someone for lunch or coffee. Explore new ideas. Meanwhile, romance is intense — even obsessive. Discussions with partners and close friends are productive.</p><h2>Scorpio (Oct. 23-Nov. 21)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Today you might become embroiled in discussions about shared property or shared costs, especially related to home and family. Now is the time to defend your own best interests related to your job, your health or a pet. You will be reasonable but firm.</p><h2>Sagittarius (Nov. 22-Dec. 21)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Get out and socialize with others today. Romantic partners will also enjoy a date today or tonight. Sports events and playful activities with kids are also excellent choices because you're in the mood to have fun. Be careful about coming on too strong.</p><h2>Capricorn (Dec. 22-Jan. 19)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Although you're focused on home, family and your private world today, this is a powerful, productive day at work for you. Financial discussions will go well. Home-related work or home-related businesses will flourish. Get busy!</p><h2>Aquarius (Jan. 20-Feb. 18)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Parents should lighten up in discussions with their kids today. Likewise, issues related to sports, the education and care of your kids, as well as romance, could be too intense. Nevertheless, this is a fun-loving, playful day. Get out and schmooze!</p><h2>Pisces (Feb. 19-March 20)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> You have the energy to make improvements at home today, especially with recycling, garbage, and plumbing/bathroom areas. Family discussions about money and finances might be an issue. Guard against extravagance. Do save your receipts.</p><h2>If today is your birthday</h2><p>Actress Keri Russell (1976) shares your birthday today. You're fascinated by what makes things tick. You're observant, analytical, and inquisitive. This is the end of a nine-year cycle. Let go of the people, places, and things from the past that held you back. Do some internal and external housecleaning. Commune with nature. Be loving and generous. </p>
entries.author: Georgia Nicols

entries.title: White Sox' Miguel Vargas working hard to become complete third baseman
entries.summary: <p>GLENDALE, Ariz. — There’s a noticeable difference in White Sox third baseman Miguel Vargas this spring.</p><p>From the way he saunters through the clubhouse and converses with teammates to his belief in his ability, Vargas is more upbeat after working through a difficult time following his trade from the Dodgers to the Sox at the deadline in 2024.</p><p>Vargas batted .104/.217/.170 in 42 games for the Sox in 2024, and his woeful stats carried over into 2025. He was batting .139 on April 19 last season.</p><p>But after a mechanical change that saw him move his hands higher on the bat, he improved. From April 19 through the end of last season, Vargas batted .248/.329/.432. His 32 doubles led the Sox and were fifth in the majors.</p><p>Vargas’ outgoing personality is more pronounced now that he has had some decent results. His uptick in performance has helped him grow into a clubhouse leader.</p><p>And he’s not content with his offensive strides. In his second full season with the Sox, Vargas said he wants to become a complete third baseman.</p><p>‘‘I’m in a better spot mentally and physically,’’ Vargas told the Sun-Times. ‘‘I want to be an every-day third baseman. That’s one of my biggest challenges. That’s one thing that I’ve been working on.’’</p><p>Offensively, he wants to shorten his droughts and has been receptive to advice from the coaching staff.</p><p>‘‘He’ll be a smartass when he needs to be — in the best of ways — but you can definitely be hard on him,’’ Sox first-year hitting coach Derek Shomon told the Sun-Times. ‘‘He receives it exceptionally well. He wants the pushback; he wants you to challenge him.’’</p><p>Vargas said that Shomon has a good feel for hitters and that he has enjoyed working with him and assistant Joel McKeithan. Going into the offseason, however, it was his defense that needed more work.</p><p>Vargas began working on his defense as soon as last season ended. He and coach Justin Jirschele frequently talked, texted and exchanged videos during the offseason. Jirschele has said Vargas wants to be a Gold Glove winner, so they focused on improving his range and being ‘‘weightless.’’</p><p>‘‘Being weightless is so that when he’s landing, he’s not driving his feet into the ground,’’ Jirschele told the Sun-Times. ‘‘When he’s in the air, he’s got all the information he needs by the time the ball is hit. Once he lands, he knows where he’s going. His feet are already turned, and it just helps him get that burst.’’</p><p>Burst and reaction time were important for Vargas as he looked to improve his range. He was the fourth-worst defender among 39 qualified third basemen in terms of outs above average last season.</p><p>The Sox are pleased with Vargas’ arm strength at third, but they also wanted him to improve his pre-pitch setup, which allows for a smoother transition coming in and going back on grounders.</p><p>In addition to the physical improvements, Jirschele said he has noticed a shift in Vargas’ mentality.</p><p>‘‘I think it’s just confidence,’’ Jirschele said.</p><p>Last season provided Vargas with a blueprint for success in the majors.</p><p>“You’re gonna fall astray, as the valley comes for everyone,’’ Shomon said. ‘‘Nobody’s ever free of that. Your ability to get out of that quicker is really knowing yourself and knowing what that looks like on a daily basis.’’</p>
entries.author: Kyle Williams

entries.title: Blackhawks notes: Nick Lardis delivers when given bigger opportunity
entries.summary: <p>Jeff Blashill finally gave <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks" target="_blank" >Blackhawks</a> rookie forward Nick Lardis an opportunity on the second line Sunday, and Lardis promptly rewarded his faith.</p><p>"That was Nick’s best game since he’s been up," Blashill said after the Hawks' <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks/2026/03/22/blackhawks-ducks-sharks-overtime-shootout-records-jeff-blashill-predators" target="_blank" >3-2 overtime loss</a> to the Predators.</p><p>Lardis scored his sixth goal of the season on a second-period rush when his attempted pass to Frank Nazar deflected in off Nicolas Hague.</p><p>The Hawks also controlled play during most of his shifts with Nazar and Bertuzzi, outshooting the Predators 6-3. His total ice time (13:28) still wasn't enormous, but it was nonetheless the highest it has been during his second NHL stint.</p><p>"I just want to show my strengths when I get that opportunity," Lardis said. "I thought I was skating pretty well tonight, [and] we generated a decent amount of chances."</p><p>Blashill has <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks/2026/03/18/blackhawks-nick-lardis-usage-jeff-blashill-connor-bedard-andre-burakovsky" target="_blank" >pushed</a> Lardis to instinctively move his feet whenever he gets the puck, and the coach agreed he took a step forward with that Sunday.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">GOAL: Nick Lardis' pass deflects in to open the scoring! <a href="https://t.co/8FrTiLN78P">pic.twitter.com/8FrTiLN78P</a></p>— BHF (@BlackhawksFocus) <a href="https://twitter.com/BlackhawksFocus/status/2035810505537839521?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">March 22, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p></p><h3><b>Frondell coming</b></h3><p>The Hawks and top prospect <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks/2026/03/21/blackhawks-anton-frondell-nhl-debut-connor-bedard-linemate-rebuild-success" target="_blank" >Anton Frondell</a> are working out flight logistics to get him over to North America. He could make his NHL debut as soon as Tuesday at the Islanders.</p><p>"Based on the level of play he's had all year, how well he played the last part of the year and the fact there's zero practice time this time of year, I'm going to throw him into the fire," Blashill said.</p><p>Blashill later said he would like to give rookie forward Ryan Greene some time at center down the stretch, which implies Frondell could take Greene's spot on Connor Bedard's line.</p><p>That would mean Andre Burakovsky, who has become a lightning rod for fan criticism due to Blashill's endless support of him despite his mere two points in his last 26 games, would keep his first-line spot alongside Bedard and Frondell.</p><h3><b>Grzelcyk injured</b></h3><p>Louis Crevier returned to the Hawks' lineup Sunday after missing Friday due to injury, but then Matt Grzelcyk suffered a bizarre upper-body injury early in the first period. Grzelcyk will miss the upcoming four-game road trip, Blashill said.</p><p>The Hawks' remaining five defensemen all ate a bunch of minutes as a result. Alex Vlasic played a season-high 26:34, Artyom Levshunov topped 24 minutes and Wyatt Kaiser — who had an atypically turnover-laden afternoon — exceeded 23.</p><p>"We had a few moments where we had self-errors that we didn’t need to have," Blashill said. "But it’s a lot of minutes and a lot of young 'D.' They overall did a good job."</p><h3><b>Oilers struggling</b></h3><p>When the Hawks traded Connor Murphy and Jason Dickinson to the Oilers, both veterans were excited about the opportunity to finally participate in the playoffs. </p><p>They probably still will, but it wouldn't seem so likely if the Oilers played in any division besides the Pacific. Entering Sunday, their 77 points ranked second in the Pacific but would've ranked fifth in the Central or seventh in the Atlantic or Metropolitan.</p><p>"We’re fortunate to play in this division," a candid Connor McDavid told reporters Saturday in Edmonton. "It’s a bit of a pillow fight right now."</p><p>The Oilers have gone a mediocre 4-4-1 since executing the two trades with the Hawks, which at least haven't been as disastrous for them as their acquisition of goalie Tristan Jarry from the Penguins earlier this season.</p><p>Murphy has one goal and a minus-five rating in the nine games, while Dickinson has one goal, one assist and a minus-two rating. Colton Dach made just three appearances before aggravating an injury.</p>
entries.author: Ben Pope

entries.title: Lebanese who persist in spite of war are my heroes
entries.summary: <p>I was born in Chicago, grew up in the suburbs and went to Notre Dame for undergraduate and law school. I completed a fellowship at Harvard University, and thought I know a thing or two about life. Like everyone, I had some great joys and some great heartbreaks. I practiced law, served in the Clinton administration, private equity, blah blah. I also have nothing short of an incredible daughter. I will spare you my heartbreaks.</p><p>My heroes growing up were baseball players, Bobby Kennedy and assorted rock stars.</p><p>A new chapter in my life was driven by a sense of regret of not going in the Peace Corps, or something like that. So, I received an appointment as a visiting professor at the <a class="Link" href="https://www.lau.edu.lb/study/schools.php" target="_blank" >Lebanese American University</a> in Beirut, where I thought I could make a difference.</p><p>I was immediately struck by the maturity of the students and their awareness of the global politics around them. This generation came of age during a civil war, COVID-19 and a bomb explosion equal to a nuclear blast. Then the <a class="Link" href="https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/israeli-strike-hits-central-beirut-after-evacuation-warning-2026-03-18/" target="_blank" >war started</a>.</p><p>Can an American parent fathom sending their children to school every day in the middle of a war taking place in their city? Would American students attend classes when you hear bombs dropping during a class? I now know what courage and faith means.</p><p>Of course, everyone is scared; no one wants to die. But the spirit of the Lebanese people is something to behold. When every logical part of their brain says, "we had enough" or "it’s time to leave" something else kicks in that you can actually feel: "The only thing I want is a better future for my children." So in that mindset, parents send their children to school, the kids show up, the professors teach, and everyone keeps focused on a future that definitely is not certain to be better.</p><p>I grew up where we did not deal with a war in our neighborhood. Wars were something that happened outside of our boundaries. Don’t get me wrong, I love my country. But what I see every day in Beirut is something different. Wanting a better life and everyone trying to pull together when the odds are so stacked against you runs through the veins of the Lebanese people. That is courage. That is perseverance. It is something I can’t say with certainty I could do.</p><p>So my heroes now are my students, their parents and my fellow faculty members. Guess we are never too old to learn a valuable lesson.</p><p><i>Joseph A. Cari Jr., Beirut</i>, <i>Lebanon</i></p><div class="RichTextSidebarModule Enhancement" data-module data-align-center><a class="AnchorLink" id="module-020000" name="module-020000"></a> <div class="RichTextModule-items RichTextBody"><h3>Give us your take</h3><br>Send letters to the editor to <a class="Link" href="mailto:letters@suntimes.com" target="_blank" >letters@suntimes.com</a>. To be considered for publication, letters must include your full name, your neighborhood or hometown and a phone number for verification purposes. Letters should be a maximum of approximately 375 words.</div> </div><p></p><h3>Harris’ words worthy of top billing </h3><p>I cannot help but think and notice something was inexplicably missing from the Sun-Times/WBEZ coverage of the <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/politics/2026/03/06/rev-jesse-jackson-chicago-service-house-of-hope-pullman-neighborhood-barack-obama-jb-pritzker" >funeral</a> of the Rev. Jesse Jackson: the vitally important message of former Vice President Kamala Harris's eulogy — particularly since the weekend that followed landed on <a class="Link" href="https://www.internationalwomensday.com/When-is-IWD" target="_blank" >International Women’s Day</a>, March 8.</p><p>Fortunately, I was able to attend the historically significant event at the House of Hope. Harris' powerful message touched on what to do when "doors" are closed for women and people of color, especially in America's political world. Her words were unforgettable, precisely because she profoundly honored Jackson's teachings to effectively and intelligently force those doors open, particularly for future generations. </p><p>Without taking anything away from your outstanding journalism, which robustly covered the eulogies of three of our former presidents, our governor and Mayor Brandon Johnson — all of whom happen to be males, like myself — it would have made a huge difference, especially for little girls and young women in Chicago, Illinois, across the country and around the world, if your coverage included a broader acknowledgment of the words (and wisdom!) by Harris. She deserves more space, as she is not only the first woman of color to be a presidential candidate of a major party, but also, and, more importantly, the first female and the first Black person to become the vice president of the United States.</p><p><i>Alejandro Lugo, Park Forest</i></p><h3>Playing to lose </h3><p>Basketball is a team sport. The players on a team play together, each striving to play at the highest level in the hope that that will help win games. Coaches support these efforts by the players, and the front office supports the coaches and the players in these efforts.</p><p>According to the basketball writers at the Sun-Times, however, the Bulls should be playing to lose. To advocate this is immoral. These writers are good at their craft, and Chicago fans would be much better off if they would find some other subject about which to write.</p><p>The ultimate culprit, of course, is the system: the NBA draft. Since the existence of the draft encourages immoral writing and decision making, it should be abolished. The NBA should follow the example of the National Women's Soccer League and <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/red-stars/2024/08/22/nwsl-players-union-reach-new-collective-bargaining-agreement" >eliminate its draft</a>.</p><p><i>Bert Rice, Edgewater</i></p><h3>Warm thoughts</h3><p>If there's one thing for certain, having lived in the Chicago area for decades, it's that things never get boring — especially when it comes to the weather.</p><p>For example, on March 9, we saw the <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/weather/2026/03/09/warm-temperatures-spring-march-chicago-storms" >thermometer exceed 70 degrees</a>, but as the week progressed, we were forced to endure hail, high winds and a snowstorm. Oh, the horror.</p><p>It is not magic, and it's by no means humorous either. It's simply early spring in the great Midwest. And with the baseball season commencing later this week, Chicagoans are preparing for a cold start to a very promising season.</p><p>In the meantime, we would all be wise to think of sunny days at Wrigley Field, suntan lotion, flip-flops and the words we've been longing to hear all winter: "Play ball!"</p><p><i>Bob Ory, Elgin</i><br></p>
entries.author: Letters to the Editor

entries.title: Dear Abby: Alcoholic ex refuses to accept it's over
entries.summary: <p><b>DEAR ABBY:</b> A former boyfriend, "Ray," is an alcoholic. He has been through rehab twice, but it didn't do any good. He won't stop. He keeps saying we'll work it out.</p><p>I don't understand why Ray can't get it through his head that I no longer want to be with him. I gave him two chances. Nobody else wants to help him either, including his family.</p><p>Ray can be controlling. He still contacts me and tells me what I need to do, just like when we were together. I don't need that kind of person in my life. I hope and pray God will send me the right person one day. Any advice you can offer? — <i>BREAKING AWAY IN ALABAMA</i></p><p><b>DEAR BREAKING AWAY:</b> Block Ray's number, and do not respond to any more messages from him. If he has a key to your place, change the locks. The next time Ray contacts you (hopefully sober), tell him there is nothing more to work out. You no longer want any contact with him, and if his stalking doesn't stop, you will report him to the authorities and get a restraining order. (Then, if it becomes necessary, do it.)</p><p><b>DEAR ABBY:</b> My sister "Fiora" lives with her daughter and family several states away from me and her other siblings. She is insisting we plan a party for her 80th birthday. Fiora wants it to be a destination party in an expensive resort area nowhere near where any of us live.</p><p>None of us is wealthy, but some of us have been good money managers and are comfortable in retirement. Fiora, on the other hand, has spent every last dime and has health issues, so she must live with her daughter. We have told her we feel if she wants this extravagant party, she should talk to her children, not to us. Is this unreasonable of us or of her? — <i>PARTIED OUT IN MISSOURI</i></p><p><b>DEAR PARTIED OUT:</b> Putting the rules of etiquette aside, Sister Fiora should not be demanding that her siblings foot the bill for a birthday extravaganza she can't afford. Tell her (as one) that you will be happy to split the bill for a more modest celebration or send her a check. The choice is hers, and the amount is something all the sibs can decide upon. Her adult children might also want to chip in as well.</p><p><b>DEAR ABBY:</b> Has anyone ever asked women on the receiving end of men's use of Viagra or other such medications how they feel about having to plan or schedule their so-called "lovemaking"? I feel it would make it just plain old sex rather than an emotional, loving act. To me, it would be better to have no sex at all than to have to plan for it. Also, how satisfying is it really to women? — <i>PHYLLIS IN INDIANA</i></p><p><b>DEAR PHYLLIS:</b> As I am sure you know, there is more to making love than popping a pill. The act and the desire for it should be mutual rather than a performance. Making love with someone a person cares about and is attracted to can be satisfying, but I think the answer to your question depends upon which participant you ask.</p><p><i>Dear Abby is written by Abigail Van Buren, also known as Jeanne Phillips, and was founded by her mother, Pauline Phillips. Contact Dear Abby at www.DearAbby.com or P.O. Box 69440, Los Angeles, CA 90069.</i></p><p>For an excellent guide to becoming a better conversationalist and a more sociable person, order "How to Be Popular." Send your name and mailing address, plus check or money order for $8 (U.S. funds) to: Dear Abby, Popularity Booklet, P.O. Box 446, Kings Mills, OH 45034-0446. (Shipping and handling are included in the price.)</p>
entries.author: Abigail Van Buren

entries.title: Steppenwolf receives grant from Sondheim Foundation for new play program
entries.summary: <p>Chicago’s Steppenwolf Theatre will reboot a program that supports new plays after receiving a grant from the Stephen Sondheim Foundation, an organization established under the will of the revered composer-lyricist.</p><p>The foundation has announced an inaugural round of cultural grant funding.</p><p>Neither Steppenwolf Theatre<b> </b>nor the Sondheim Foundation would disclose the amount of the grant.</p><p>The Sondheim Foundation said its mission is to support playwrights, lyricists and composers for the theater. Awardees were selected by the founding board of directors, who were close collaborators and confidantes appointed by Sondheim in his will.</p><p>“We are so honored that the Sondheim Foundation considered our program to be one where Sondheim's legacy could be continued,” said Brooke Flanagan, executive director of Steppenwolf.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/8d10949/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4715x2646+0+289/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc8%2Fbe%2F6938070c4c89bf524d55f38331d1%2Fap25176423327743.jpg data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>American multi-award-winning composer and lyricist, Stephen Sondheim poses with his framed ‘Copy of Freedom’, after being awarded the Freedom of the City of London at a ceremony at the Guildhall in London, Sept. 27, 2018. The Stephen Sondheim Foundation announced an inaugural round of awards to arts groups in March 2026. </p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Kirsty Wigglesworth/AP</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>Reached Friday, she said the grant will go toward rebuilding Steppenwolf’s Scout program, which supports new works by emerging writers and was shuttered during the COVID-19 pandemic. Steppenwolf has developed and launched more than 130 plays in its 50-year history.</p><p>The Scout program partners playwrights with Steppenwolf’s new play development team. The writer receives access to ensemble artists.</p><p>“Over the course of a year, the playwright not only meets with the team to get counsel on the process of writing for an ensemble like Steppenwolf, but also gets coaching and feedback,” Flanagan said. “Then over the course of drafts, the culminating and crucible for the process is a week-long workshop that has a director, a dramaturg, a team of actors, and finally, a staged reading in our 1700 theater.”</p><p>Flanagan said the last play to go through the program was “Mosque4Mosque” by Omer Abbas Salem. In 2021, the final reading was hosted via Zoom due to the pandemic shutdown.</p><p>The program will return for the theater’s 2026-2027 season.</p><p>“We're excited not only to bring the program back, but also doing it with our artistic directors Audrey Francis and Glenn Davis, and our director of new play development, Jonathan Green, to work to evolve it, and to make it an even stronger program,” said Flanagan.</p><p>The program is meant to stand alone, she said, so there is no guarantee that plays that make it through the program will be staged as full productions at Steppenwolf. But there have been a few plays in the past, including “Bald Sisters” by Vichet Chum, that have been produced in full after beginning in the Scout program.</p><p>Flanagan said it has not been discussed whether or not the funding will continue past the 2026-27 season.</p><p>“I think we are really thrilled to be able to have the program back up and running, and we know that we're looking forward to the collaboration and what it means to the industry and to the artists that are involved,” she said.</p><p>The Playwrights’ Center of Minneapolis, Millay Arts, and Rhinebeck Writers Retreat also received grants from the Sondheim Foundation in the inaugural round.</p><p>Sondheim, who died in 2021 at 91, had a long career that included winning Academy, Tony and Grammy awards, as well as the Pulitzer Prize for Drama (for “Sunday in the Park with George”). His most popular musicals include “Sweeney Todd” and “Into the Woods.”<br></p>
entries.author: Mike Davis | WBEZ

entries.title: Chicago-area Lebanese Americans mourn dead, try to help loved ones as 1 million displaced by Israeli strikes
entries.summary: <p>Three of Rodolph Saliba’s friends in Lebanon have been killed by rockets in recent weeks, the 19-year-old Oak Lawn resident said. Even more are sleeping in tents or going hungry.</p><p>Israeli strikes have killed at least 1,500 people in Iran and more than 1,000 in Lebanon after the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah fired on Israel, while Iranian strikes have killed at least 15 in Israel, according to <a class="Link" href="https://apnews.com/article/iran-iraq-us-israel-trump-march-19-2026-52e94398f2432b3aba9b02b51fbe5000" target="_blank" >officials in those countries</a>. Israeli fire has displaced more than a million people in Lebanon, or roughly 20% of the population just over a year since <a class="Link" href="https://news.un.org/en/story/2024/09/1155141" target="_blank" >the last conflict uprooted a million Lebanese</a> residents from their homes.</p><p>Thousands of Lebanese Americans — about <a class="Link" href="https://www.aaiusa.org/s/AA-demographics-ILLINOIS-3.pdf" target="_blank" >15,000 in Illinois</a>, the state with the <a class="Link" href="https://www.aaiusa.org/demographics" target="_blank" >sixth-largest Arab population</a>, according to Arab American Institute estimates and Census data — are watching their home country come under fire as the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran continues to ignite the region.</p><p>“They do not deserve this,” said Saliba, who moved to Oak Lawn from Lebanon eight months ago. “There are a lot of innocent people who don't deserve this... I just hope this war stops.”</p><p>Conflict has been raging since the U.S. and Israel assassinated Iranian leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in joint strikes that also wiped out dozens of other leaders Feb. 28. President Donald Trump said the aim was to destroy Iran’s naval and missile capabilities, and to prevent the country from obtaining a nuclear weapon. Hezbollah <a class="Link" href="https://apnews.com/article/lebanon-israel-hezbollah-shiites-base-criticism-84d6e421181c33aac2dee8c42ecdcb9c" target="_blank" >resumed rocket fire</a> on Israel days later, about 15 months after the group and Israel signed a ceasefire agreement ending months of conflict.</p><p>Like Saliba and others, Muhammad Sankari, a Southwest Side resident, has spent weeks checking in on family and friends. The government has only been able to accommodate roughly 120,000 displaced people as it scrambles to open shelters, the Associated Press reported. </p><p>Sankari, an organizer with the U.S. Palestinian Community Network, was last in Lebanon in 2012, and hopes to return soon. Saliba was also planning to go this summer, “war or no war." Both now worry about returning.</p><p>“It's a country full of some of the best memories of my childhood,” Sankari said. "It’s difficult to be so far removed when your loved ones are experiencing these horrors… Even if the bombs stop tomorrow, there's a huge economic and humanitarian impact.”</p><p>For Saliba, the bombardment feels all too familiar.</p><p>He was born in the last days of the 2006 July War, which <a class="Link" href="https://apnews.com/article/what-is-hezbollahisrael-lebanon-b4daa0a6084df27099cef45b59120034" target="_blank" >lasted a month</a> after Hezbollah fighters ambushed an Israeli patrol at the border and took two Israeli soldiers hostage. It <a class="Link" href="https://apnews.com/article/israel-hezbollah-ceasefire-united-nations-resolution-1701-86e3668220e8dbdb390bc803004e9660" target="_blank" >ended in a ceasefire</a>, but Israeli attacks wrought destruction in southern Lebanon.</p><p>So when an <a class="Link" href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SWwETl5hkLA" target="_blank" ><u>overnight Israeli strike</u></a> killed at least eight people and wounded more than 30 in Beirut’s Ramlet el-Bayda neighborhood last week, he "wasn't shocked." He remembered being accustomed to the violence. Among other tragedies, he says he had witnessed a young boy and his father mutilated by <a class="Link" href="https://apnews.com/projects/israel-gaza-war-pager-attack-survivors-hezbollah/" target="_blank" ><u>Israeli pager bombs</u></a> in 2024 that killed more than a dozen and wounded thousands.</p><p>“I was born in 2006,” he said. “<a class="Link" href="https://casebook.icrc.org/case-study/israellebanonhezbollah-conflict-2006" target="_blank" >They blew the hospital up [where I was born]</a>… I have seen missiles drop 15 meters away from me. You’ll see an F-45 over you and the restaurants and mechanics are still open. They have to make a living.”</p><p>Karl El Sokhn, an Albany Park resident, was 12 at the time of the July War, and remembers seeing those explosions in the distance. While his family has largely been spared over the years due to living near the U.S. embassy, he said his parents' friends now sleep in their car after being displaced.</p><p>Like Saliba, the now 32-year-old moved to the U.S. from Lebanon as a teen. Leaving home was expected for Lebanese youth due to the frequent wars with Israel, he said, and made more painful given "there’s been foreign influence."</p><p>“The idea of having to leave your home is kind of impossible to explain,” El Sokhn said. “[And yet] it’s just normal for the youth to grow up and become a diaspora.”</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/02ea520/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3000x1684+0+158/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F33%2Ff9%2F552b3d7943d184d8e607d494fbe9%2Flebanonreact-260323-5.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/e791920/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3000x1684+0+158/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F33%2Ff9%2F552b3d7943d184d8e607d494fbe9%2Flebanonreact-260323-5.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Karl El Sokhn, a Lebanese Albany Park resident, walks through his neighborhood Friday, March 20, 2026. Sokhn has family and friends currently living in Lebanon who are enduring the Israeli bombardment.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Tyler Pasciak LaRiviere/Sun-Time</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>In December, El Sokhn, a multi-instrumentalist, played<b> </b>in a tribute show at Chop Shop in Wicker Park for the late Ziad Rahbini, a Lebanese musician who wrote about, among other topics, <a class="Link" href="https://www.nytimes.com/1982/06/07/world/why-israelis-invaded-now.html" target="_blank" >Israel’s 1982 invasion</a>. It was his way of trying to fight Islamophobia and anti-Arab sentiment in the U.S.</p><p>“You preserve your culture and share it with people, you’re re-humanizing because we’re all being dehumanized,” El Sokhn said.</p><p></p><p>Now, more Israeli ground troops have been sent into Lebanon. Israel's military chief of staff, Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir, said it's “determined to deepen the operation until all of our objectives are achieved,” the Associated Press reported. Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam has called on President Donald Trump to hold talks with Israel for an immediate ceasefire, noting that more than 500 Hezbollah military positions and weapons depots have been dismantled, <a class="Link" href="https://www.cnn.com/2026/03/19/middleeast/lebanese-prime-minister-exclusive-interview-latam-intl" target="_blank" >CNN reported</a>.</p><p>“Lebanon did not choose this war,” Salam said, <a class="Link" href="https://apnews.com/live/iran-war-israel-trump-03-13-2026" target="_blank" >criticizing Hezbollah’s rocket fire</a> into Israel. “There is no justification in holding an entire nation hostage.”</p><p>Saliba only expects Lebanon to be spared when Hezbollah lays down its arms or is wiped out by Israel — so "this is never going to stop.” Meanwhile, he has been sending money and doing what he can from afar to help arrange shelter for friends.</p><p>Sankari said Americans had a responsibility to stop the "war machine" through economic and political pressure because “war is big business.”</p><p>“Our duty is to dig in and fight here domestically to stop [the United States'] complicity and role in the death of innocents,” Sankari said. “Billions have been spent killing people in this short period of time, in Palestine, Lebanon and Iran, and continues to be spent while people here are suffering greatly.”</p><p><i>Contributing: Araceli Gómez-Aldana, WBEZ</i></p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li with ties to Iran, Middle East worry as U.S., Israel continue bombing</a> </li> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" entries.title: Where to see Axolotls in Chicago
entries.summary: <p>My son could not have been more excited, shouting across the aquarium to come look. Was it a turtle? A small shark?</p><p>When I meandered over to the aquarium window inside the Little Red Schoolhouse Nature Center in Willow Springs, I found what had captured his attention: a strange swimming white blob with fins behind its head.</p><p>I had never heard of the creature. Perhaps you haven’t either?</p><p>Allow me to introduce the axolotl, an amphibian and also all the rage at the moment of many Chicago classroom conversations. Leaping into the spotlight thanks to their quirky charm, regenerative superpowers and a starring role in the popular video game Minecraft, they are a budding star at local aquariums.</p><p>As the popularity of the axolotl has grown, attention is trickling down through multiple age ranges, with books and stuffed animals, even a ceramic paint set and stickers at Target.</p><p>Like many families, after we saw one and our child — and OK, also maybe a parent or two too — became obsessed, down an aquatic rabbit hole we went.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-390000" name="html-embed-module-390000"></a><div class="flourish-embed flourish-map" data-src="visualisation/28156847"><script src="https://public.flourish.studio/resources/embed.js"></script><noscript><img src="https://public.flourish.studio/visualisation/28156847/thumbnail" width="100%" alt="map visualization" /></noscript></div> </div> </div><p>We got the graphic novel “Max, a Little Axolotl #1,” by Joey Spiotto. We added our request to the lines for the books “My Life at the Bottom: the Story of a Lonesome Axolotl” (number 2 in line for 7 copies) and “Axoltol: A First Field Guide to the Amphibian That Never Grows Up,” (number 3 in line for 29 copies) at the Chicago Public Library.</p><p>Once you start looking, you see them everywhere. We bought the “Max” book at our local bookstore, which also had finger-puppet axolotls available.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-360000" name="image-360000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/18d7989/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F50%2Fce%2Fbdea81bb4adc9069305c2147b0f8%2Faxolotlhunt-260323-07.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/1259e09/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F50%2Fce%2Fbdea81bb4adc9069305c2147b0f8%2Faxolotlhunt-260323-07.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/f1e74fe/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F50%2Fce%2Fbdea81bb4adc9069305c2147b0f8%2Faxolotlhunt-260323-07.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="A male axolotl, deemed “Sir Mix-O-Lotl” through a naming contest, swims in his tank at Brookfield Zoo, Thursday, March 19, 2026. Sir Mix-O-Lotl was one of 20 rescued axolotls confiscated by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service because they were illegally brought through O’Hare International Airport from Indonesia." srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/f1e74fe/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F50%2Fce%2Fbdea81bb4adc9069305c2147b0f8%2Faxolotlhunt-260323-07.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/f1e74fe/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F50%2Fce%2Fbdea81bb4adc9069305c2147b0f8%2Faxolotlhunt-260323-07.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>This male axolotl was deemed “Sir Mix-O-Lotl” after a naming contest at Brookfield Zoo.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Ashlee Rezin/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>And we found other places in the Chicago area to add to our list to be checked off on our axolotl adventure — an axolotl is on display at the Pilcher Park Nature Center in Joliet, confirmed Nature Center Superintendent Katie Zaban.</p><p>And at the Brookfield Zoo, which began displaying one axolotl in a habitat last year, it’s an even bigger hit than the penguins right across from it, said Mike Masellis, lead animal care specialist for aquatics.</p><p>“It’s fun to see someone look at a penguin and then scream about an axolotl," he said.</p><p>Last year, the Brookfield Zoo took in 20 rescued axolotls after they were confiscated by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service as they were illegally brought through O’Hare from Indonesia.</p><p>In November, the zoo posted a Facebook video about the new animal, calling them the "Peter Pans of salamanders."</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="FacebookUrl"> <script>(function(d, s, id) { var js, fjs = d.getElementsByTagName(s)[0]; if (d.getElementById(id)) return; js = d.createElement(s); js.id = id; js.src = "//connect.facebook.net/en_US/sdk.js#xfbml=1&version=v20.0"; fjs.parentNode.insertBefore(js, fjs); }(document, 'script', 'facebook-jssdk'));</script><div class="fb-post" data-href="https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=1120332483421380"></div> </div> </div> </div></div><p>Through a naming contest, one was deemed “Sir Mix-O-Lotl;” fans quickly arrived. (The other rescues are not on display or were transferred to other organizations.)</p><p>Through my adventures, I now know that the axolotl is a critically endangered amphibian; they originate from Lake Xochimilco and Lake Chalco near Mexico City, according to the Lincoln Park Zoo.</p><p>Among the other facts I could now rattle off? Although amphibian — typically living on both water and land — the axolotl spends all its time in the water. They like to eat earthworms. They remain in a larval stage forever, never becoming full adults.</p><p>“They’re just kind of really cool animals,” Masellis said. “Maybe that’s why kids love them so much – they're like children at heart.”</p><p>And, of course, coolest of all, it can regenerate parts of its body, fast. Masellis estimates the animal could regenerate a foot in a month. Wow!</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-e20000" name="image-e20000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/587f271/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F0a%2F27%2F325c0f574973ade5e168cb0edc68%2Faxolotlhunt-260323-09.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/f80d161/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F0a%2F27%2F325c0f574973ade5e168cb0edc68%2Faxolotlhunt-260323-09.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/09bf71a/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F0a%2F27%2F325c0f574973ade5e168cb0edc68%2Faxolotlhunt-260323-09.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="A male axolotl, deemed “Sir Mix-O-Lotl” through a naming contest, swims in his tank at Brookfield Zoo, Thursday, March 19, 2026. Sir Mix-O-Lotl was one of 20 rescued axolotls confiscated by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service because they were illegally brought through O’Hare International Airport from Indonesia. " srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/09bf71a/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F0a%2F27%2F325c0f574973ade5e168cb0edc68%2Faxolotlhunt-260323-09.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/8866118/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F0a%2F27%2F325c0f574973ade5e168cb0edc68%2Faxolotlhunt-260323-09.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/09bf71a/2147483647/strip/true/crop/2500x1403+0+132/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F0a%2F27%2F325c0f574973ade5e168cb0edc68%2Faxolotlhunt-260323-09.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Axolotls stay in a larval stage forever, never becoming full adults.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Ashlee Rezin/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>As for our family, determined to find another axolotl, we made a plan to visit the Lincoln Park Zoo after school one day, where the axolotl’s entry popped up pretty quickly under “Most Popular” on the zoo map.</p><p>But after parking, I made a grave parental error, walking us past the seals into an area near the Pritzker Family Children’s Zoo that has no exit. The area is enclosed to trap children and keep them safe from the nearby street traffic but also encloses parents with poor senses of direction.</p><p>By the time we regrouped and emerged in the right direction, spotting the Regenstein Small Mammal-Reptile House and reaching for the doors, we found them — gasp! — locked. At 4:38 p.m., I’d made the rookie mistake of forgetting that many of the zoo’s buildings close early. My son, who is learning to read, sounded out the sign: “Closes 30 minutes before the zoo closes.” </p><p>We trudged back to the car.</p><p>A few days later, we went back. Walking into the reptile house, victory was ours — a gray-shaded axolotl was waiting for us in a prime spot, just near the door. Both of my kids bent down to look at it, peering through the glass.</p><p>“Mom,” my son asked me, "Wouldn't it be cool to have an axolotl as a pet, so we could see it every day?”</p><p>Indeed, although this is something zookeepers very much dissuade, as axolotls should be cared for by experts who are familiar with their unique selves.</p><p>Instead, we’ll have multiple Chicago options to keep seeing them all.</p>
entries.author: Alison Bowen

entries.title: Review: ‘Madama Butterfly’ at Lyric Opera is a bold, new take on Puccini’s beloved opera
entries.summary: <p>Giacomo Puccini’s “Madama Butterfly,” one of the most popular and frequently performed operas in the world, is a searing story of the exploitation of a teenage geisha set against a backdrop of imperialist ignorance and hubris.</p><p>But sometimes forgotten or overlooked is that the 1904 opera itself is a kind of exploitation as well — white, European creators and interpreters offering their perhaps well-meaning yet frequently ill-informed re-creation of Japanese culture both in the music and story.</p><p>“Just as Butterfly is trapped with little agency in this opera, we as Asian Americans have been trapped by many of the traditional depictions of Butterfly’s story,” writes stage director Matthew Ozawa in his program note.</p><div class="RatingCard Enhancement" data-module data-rating="2.5" data-align-center> <div class="RatingCard-title"> ‘Madama Butterfly’ </div> <div class="RatingCard-content"> <span class="RatingCard-star"> <span class="RatingCard-star-full"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> <span class="RatingCard-star-half"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> </span> <span class="RatingCard-star"> <span class="RatingCard-star-full"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> <span class="RatingCard-star-half"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> </span> <span class="RatingCard-star"> <span class="RatingCard-star-full"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> <span class="RatingCard-star-half"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> </span> <span class="RatingCard-star"> <span class="RatingCard-star-full"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> <span class="RatingCard-star-half"> <svg class=""> <use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use> </svg> </span> </span> </div> </div><p></p><div class="RichTextSidebarModule Enhancement" data-module data-align-center><a class="AnchorLink" id="module-290000" name="module-290000"></a> <div class="RichTextModule-items RichTextBody"><b>When:</b> 2 p.m. March 22 with six additional performances through April 12<br><b>Where:</b> Lyric Opera House, 20 N. Wacker Drive<br><b>Tickets:</b> $55-$434<br><b>Info:</b> 312-827-5600; <a class="Link" href="lyricopera.org/butterfly" target="_blank" >lyricopera.org/butterfly</a></div> </div><p>He and an entirely Japanese and Japanese American artistic team have conceived a bold, new take on “Madama Butterfly” that was first seen at the Cincinnati Opera in 2023 and is being presented for the first time at Lyric Opera of Chicago in a nine-performance run ending April 12.</p><p>But as commendable as the intent and imagination behind this version are, the intrinsic emotional power of this beloved opera is significantly lost amid the production’s complex, conceptual structure and invented narrative overlay.</p><p>In this version, the opera opens in a present-day apartment where B.F. Pinkerton dons a virtual reality headset and enters a game that sets the whole story in motion with him in the role of his avatar — a Navy lieutenant at the beginning of the 20th century arriving in Nagasaki aboard his warship, the Abraham Lincoln.</p><p>There is a frequent, often jarring and no doubt intentional collision between past and present, between Pinkerton’s contemporary world and his virtual reality imaginings, with him sometimes hovering bizarrely and distractingly on the sidelines when he is not directly involved in the action in his avatar role.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-d30000" name="image-d30000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source
